zh.Bridge Sollers, Herefordshire, England: 英國英格蘭賀福爾布里奇 索萊爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Simon de Brugge II於1270年出生在英國賀福爾布里奇 索萊爾時，他的父親Sir Simon de Brugge I - Lord of the Manor27歲，母親Juliana Walwyn30歲。他於大約1294年與Mrs White在英國英格蘭賀福爾布里奇 索萊爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1323年在英國英格蘭賀福爾布里奇 索萊爾享年53歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Simon de Brugge II于1270年出生在英国賀福爾布里奇 索萊爾时，他的父亲Sir Simon de Brugge I - Lord of the Manor27岁，母亲Juliana Walwyn30岁。他于大约1294年与Mrs White在英国英格兰賀福爾布里奇 索萊爾结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1323年在英国英格兰賀福爾布里奇 索萊爾享年53岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Brugge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch (Van der Brugge): topographic name for someone living near a brug ‘bridge’.
Flemish and Dutch: habitational name from Bruges.
North German (Brügge): variant of Bruck or a habitational name from any of several places called Brügge.

variants.name: Bruck
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Naftali, Eliahu, Hershel, Meyer, Ruvim, Shloime, Yoav.

German (also Brück): topographic name for someone who lived near a bridge, or an occupational name for a bridge keeper or toll collector on a bridge, from Middle High German bruck(e) ‘bridge’. Bruck remained the Bavarian and Austrian form, reflected in placenames such as Innsbruck, whereas in central and northern Germany Brück(e) was the regular and ultimately standard form, as for example in the placename Osnabrück. Switzerland has both forms. See also Brueck .
Jewish (from Poland and Belarus): from Polish, Belorussian, or Yiddish bruk ‘pavement’, possibly a metonymic occupational name for a paver.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: Simon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

variants.name: Simen
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Simon de Brugge II was born in 1270, in Bridge Sollers, Herefordshire, England, his father, Sir Simon de Brugge I - Lord of the Manor, was 27 and his mother, Juliana Walwyn, was 30. He married Mrs White about 1294, in Bridge Sollers, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1323, in Bridge Sollers, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 53.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Simon de Brugge II est né en 1270, à Bridge Sollers, Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Simon de Brugge I - Lord of the Manor, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Juliana Walwyn, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Mrs White environ 1294, à Bridge Sollers, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1323, à Bridge Sollers, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 53 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Simon de Brugge IIが1270年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアブリッジ ソラーズで生まれた時，父Sir Simon de Brugge I - Lord of the Manorは27歳，母Juliana Walwynは30歳であった。約1294年にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアブリッジ ソラーズでMrs Whiteと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1323年にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアブリッジ ソラーズで53歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Simon de Brugge II(이)가 1270년 영국 허퍼드실르 브리지 솔레르스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Simon de Brugge I - Lord of the Manor(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Juliana Walwyn(은)는 30세였다. 그는 대략 1294년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 브리지 솔레르스에서 Mrs White(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1323년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 브리지 솔레르스에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Simon de Brugge II родился в 1270 г. в местности бридж соллирс, хирефордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Simon de Brugge I - Lord of the Manor было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Juliana Walwyn, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs White около 1294 г. в местности бридж соллирс, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1323 г. в местности бридж соллирс, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 53 лет.
